questions.prompt: Report the question: "Do you like coffee?" → She asked me ___ coffee.
questions.explanation: Yes/no reported questions use 'if' or 'whether' and restore subject-verb order with tense backshift: 'if I liked'. Option (a) keeps the question inversion and present tense. Option (c) keeps the present tense 'like' and so misses the required backshift to 'liked'. Option (d) wrongly keeps the auxiliary 'did' after 'whether'.

questions.prompt: Report the command: "Sit down!" → The teacher told the students ___.
questions.explanation: Reported commands use 'tell/ask + object + to-infinitive': 'told the students to sit down'. Option (b) keeps the bare imperative form, which is only correct in direct speech. Option (c) uses 'that' before a bare imperative, which is wrong. Option (d) uses the gerund, which doesn't report a command.

questions.prompt: Report the question: "What time does the film start?" → He asked me ___.

options.text: what time did the film start
what time does the film start

questions.explanation: Reported wh-questions restore subject-verb order and backshift: 'what time the film started'. Option (a) keeps the auxiliary 'did' after the wh-word, which is not used in reported questions. Option (c) keeps the present tense and auxiliary 'does' — no backshift, no statement order. Option (d) inverts the subject and verb incorrectly.

questions.prompt: Report the request: "Please don't make any noise." → She asked them ___.
questions.explanation: Negative reported commands use 'ask/tell + object + not to + infinitive': 'asked them not to make any noise'. The 'not' comes before 'to', not after. Option (a) keeps the direct imperative form. Option (b) incorrectly splits the infinitive by placing 'not' between 'to' and 'make'. Option (d) uses a 'that' clause which loses the command structure.

questions.prompt: Report the question: "Are you coming to the party?" → He asked her ___.

options.text: was she coming to the party
if she was coming to the party
if was she coming to the party
whether she comes to the party

questions.explanation: Yes/no reported questions use 'if/whether' + subject-verb order + backshift: 'if she was coming to the party'. Option (a) is missing 'if/whether' and keeps the inverted order. Option (c) keeps the inverted word order after 'if'. Option (d) has the correct structure but forgets to backshift ('comes' should be 'was coming').

options.text: She asked me if I had finished my homework.
He asked me where I had been all day.
The coach told us not to give up.
She asked me did I want some tea.

questions.explanation: Option (d) is wrong: after 'asked me', the reported question must use 'if/whether' and restore statement order — 'She asked me if I wanted some tea'. Keeping 'did' with inverted order is a direct speech structure and cannot appear in reported speech. Options (a), (b), and (c) are all correctly formed.
